title: Rich British Sardine Salad
description: A silky, jammy, lazy‑Sunday British dish that blends roasted sardines, fruit jam, and a touch of spherification for a sophisticated palate.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and lay sardines on a baking sheet, drizzle with 2 tbsp olive oil and bake for 8 minutes until lightly crisp.
While sardines cook, combine soaked apricots, honey, lemon zest, and lemon juice in a small saucepan, simmer until thick, then cool to room temperature.
Dissolve sodium alginate in 200 ml lukewarm water using a hand blender until completely smooth; let stand for 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl, dissolve calcium lactate in a small amount of water to create a 1% solution.
Once sardines are done, remove from oven, allow to cool slightly, then break into bite‑sized pieces.
Using a syringe, drop small spheres of the apricot jam into the sodium alginate bath to create spherified pearls; let sit for 2 minutes before draining.
Place sardine pieces on a serving plate, drizzle with apple cider vinegar and the remaining olive oil.
Sprinkle the spherified jam pearls over the sardines, then season with sea salt and black pepper.
Garnish with a light sprinkle of fresh lemon zest for brightness.
Serve immediately as a silky, jammy British salad perfect for a lazy Sunday.
